title: -Referral Response Alternative Housing Options for People Experiencing Homelessness

sections.current_situation:
City Council’s May 20 referral to the City Manager requested a list of sites, both owned
by the City and available for purchase or lease on the private market, that could be used
to identify additional shelter sites for people experiencing unsheltered homelessness in
Berkeley. The Council’s direction in that referral was to identify sites that could be
feasible for replicating the Horizon Transitional Village Program shelter and SPARK
safe parking model (previously co-located on Grayson St.), as well as sites that could
host other models deemed successful by staff.
With this in mind, staff have compiled the following information in this report:
 Attachment 1 is a compilation of private market listings of all commercial and
industrial properties that offer a minimum of 5,000 sq ft.
 Attachment 2 is a compilation of motels/hotels currently available for lease or
purchase;
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@CityofBerkeley.info Website: http://www.CityofBerkeley.info/Manager
Page 2 of 20
Referral Response: Alternative Housing Options for Homelessness INFORMATION REPORT
October 14, 2025
 Attachment 3 is a compilation of vacant city-owned land and city-owned
properties, excluding the following types of property:
o City parks, as these were removed from the Council referral;
o City facilities actively used by staff (e.g., fire stations, the Animal Shelter,
the Civic Center building at 2180 Milvia St, the Public Safety Building,
etc.);
o Any housing owned by the City or BHA that is currently occupied.
Absent further direction from Council as to appropriate property types, locations, and
preferred programs, staff are not in a position to provide any recommendations as to
any potentially viable individual properties. With that said:
 If Council’s desire is to replicate a Grayson-style shelter program, then staff
would recommend initially performing due diligence at properties that are
de
sections.background:
On May 20, 2025, the City Council passed a referral to the City Manager, “Alternative
Housing Options for People Experiencing Homelessness.” This referral had multiple
parts, and the current report is responsive to one part of this referral. Staff actions in
response to other aspects of this referral include:
 On May 28, the City Manager sent a letter on behalf of the Council to the
Alameda County Board of Supervisors, affirming the four policy principles
outlined in the May 20, 2025 referral.
 On July 24, 2025, Council voted to support staff recommendations in the report,
“Recommendations for County Measure W Funding.” The adoptions of these
recommendations resulted in a letter sent to the Alameda County Board of
Supervisors outlining Berkeley’s priorities for County Measure W sales tax
funding.
 On July 29, 2025, at a special meeting, Council received a report from staff,
“Comprehensive Summary of Berkeley’s Homeless Response System.” This
report responded to several aspects of Council’s May 20, 2025 referral, including
an overview of ongoing efforts to address homelessness; a resource needs and
gaps analysis of the homeless system; a feasibility and cost analysis to support
new homeless programs; and a geographic assessment of homeless service
locations in the city. Due to technical difficulties, that special meeting was
adjourned prior to the conclusion of staff’s presentation on the report. The
presentation was concluded during a special meeting on September 16, 2025.
 On October 28, 2025, the Council will consider a recommendation from the City
Manager to send a letter to Alameda County Supervisor Fortunato Bas regarding
opportunities to partner on regional solutions to unsheltered homelessness.
Page 5 of 20
Referral Response: Alternative Housing Options for Homelessness INFORMATION REPORT
October 14, 2025
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS

sections.preamble:
Page 1 of 20
Office of the City Manager
INFORMATION CALENDAR
OCTOBER 14, 2025
To: Honorable Mayor and Members of the City Council
From: Paul Buddenhagen, City Manager
Submitted by: Peter Radu, Assistant to the City Manager
Subject: Referral Response: Alternative Housing Options for People Experiencing
Homelessness
INTRODUCTION
This report responds in part to the City Council’s May 20, 2025 referral, “Alternative
Housing Options for People Experiencing Homelessness,” referring to the City Manager
“a request for staff to identify a list of potential locations of City-owned and private
properties (to be leased or acquired) – for the purpose of establishing 24/7 staffed
shelter sites, including but not limited to indoor/outdoor camping areas, safe RV and car
park zones, and/or congregate or non-congregate shelters for people experiencing
homelessness based on the model used at Grayson Street and other models deemed
successful by the City Manager.”
